rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNE SHIRLEY OUTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/9/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: There was no bank statement appended to the petition or filed separately. (The original statement, not a photocopy, should have been part of the e-filed petition.) (Schedule D-1 of the prior accounting did not include two CD accounts with Bank of America. No supplemental I&A was filed or other support for the escheated assets received.) Who is the deceased executor's successor-in-interest, who would be entitled, eventually, to some portion of the statutory compensation? Such person (personal representative of his estate or trustee of his trust) is entitled to notice. Mai Findlay is the named executor of Mabel Cocking's will. Nothing has been filed to evidence the will having been admitted to probate in the UK and Mai Findlay having been appointed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 4/27/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JENNIE ACTKINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/20/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please address Probate Code 2620 (b). It appears the account needs to be amended to include a supplemental account starting after the date of death. 2) It appears the court cannot grant cost or attorney fees based on an estimated expense. Please explain the legal basis / cite probate code sections for the court awarding costs and attorney fees based on estimated expenses. 3) Conservator of the estate please file timesheets that conform with LRSF 14.52. Local Rule 14.81(A) is cited on page 6 line 23 but LRSF 14.81(A) not a Local Rule. 4) The notice GC-020 does not include the required information regarding remote appearances as ordered in the Tenth Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division. Matter will be continued to allow time to provide proper notice. 5) Please address whether court appointed attorney Dennis Mesina will be petitioning for fees and address whether the account can close without addressing his fees. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 04/04/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JENNIE ACTKINSON TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/20/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The notice does not include the required information regarding remote appearances as ordered in the Tenth Amended General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division. Matter will be continued to allow time to provide proper notice. 2) Trustee please file both ordinary and extraordinary timesheets that conform with LRSF 14.52. The court may not grant a request for extraordinary fees without timesheets that show the ordinary services. 3) Please explain why the $4,016.40 in property management fees already paid have not been reduced from the requested trustee fee when managing trust property is a trustee duty. 4) The citi bank statement verify balance as of 10/31/2021. Please provide a bank statement that verifies the balance as of the end of the account 11/9/2021. 5) Please provide receipts for all $6,317.39 in requested reimbursements. If no receipt is available please explain the legal basis / cite probate code for the court granting reimbursement with no receipt. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 04/04/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF OMAR ABDULLAH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Establishing Special Needs Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Notice of this petition was given to the Directors of State Hospitals and Developmental Services at their old addresses. The conservator explains that all mailed correspondence to each agency is forwarded to the new address. The documents mailed to both agencies have not been returned as undeliverable. 2) No proof of service has been filed to show a copy of the conservator's supplemental declaration has been served on the state agencies. CRC 7.53(b) & Probate Code section 3602(f). 3) The conservator's supplemental declaration eliminated a trust provision which required a protector. The conservator explains that the provision was inadvertently included. 4) The petition requests a $30,000 bond for the trustee with an order requiring the balance of the net settlement proceeds to be placed in a blocked account in lieu of a full bond. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Substitute Personal Representatives Jennifer Corry And Colleen Corry Kellison In Place Of Deceased Respondent Eamon Corry Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/27/2022. Third continuance same issues. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please file a memorandum of points and authorities explaining why a petition was filed when the Code of Civil Procedure 377.31 says, "on motion..." It appears this should be filed as a motion on the law and motion court calendar. 2) Please file an actual memorandum of points and authorities in support of the petition. 3) Notice is not proper. Please give 30 day's notice to all parties not just the attorneys in the matter. (Probate Code 1202). An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 04/11/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

Appearance required, although notice appears to be defective: no direct notice to the would-be respondent(s); incorrect address for William Monroe; and incomplete caption shown on the face of the Notice of Hearing. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM H. GREEN REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Title To Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/11/2022. Fourth continuance for same issues at request of counsel. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1. Venue is a threshold issue that must be determined prior to the petition. Please explain and provide the legal basis /cite probate code sections for why San Francisco is the proper venue when the trustee appears to live in Edgewood, MD. 2. Please file evidence showing the Wells Fargo bank account ending in 9287 was in existence prior to execution of the trust. 3. Please file a current account statement of the bank account. 4. Please explain all the Decedent's heirs so the court can determine whether notice is proper under Prob. C. 851 (b). 5. The proposed order does not match what is requested in the prayer/request in the petition. It appears the petition must be amended to match what is requested in the proposed order as the prayer/request in the petition does not indicate the account number or where the bank account is and just says "Directing that the Bank Account is an asset of the William H. Green Revocable Trust." Also the second page of the proposed order "That the parties shall execute all documents and perform such additional acts as may be necessary to carry out the above" is not in the petition's prayer/request. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 03/28/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ALLA REYKHEL LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Co-Trustees And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/25/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please file a copy of the 16061.7 notice and a copy of the trust bank account statement that shows the address or any other documents that will allow the court to determine venue is proper in San Francisco. 2) Please explain the redaction of Wells Fargo account numbers on the trust page 10 and 14. 3) Please file evidence showing the alleged Wells Fargo accounts were in existence prior to execution of the trust. 4) The DE-115 does not show the required 30 days notice given and no waivers of notice filed. 5) There appears to be no steps taken by Decedent to transfer 1900 Cedar Street, Calistoga, CA to the trust after the real property was granted to Decedent in 2014. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/11/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD FRANCISCO BASANES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/11/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please file form DE-147 that includes all required information including the proposed personal representative's phone number. 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 3) Please must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 3/28/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
